validation_output.process_scan: No lingering launchdeck/dev-server/ready-http/process-alive/external-server fixture processes found; only the PowerShell scan command matched.

consumer_evidence: events --json reads bounded structured event history for a registered project target.
logs --follow --json emits visible schemaVersion=1 JSON Lines with type=log.line, projectId, task, runId, timestamp, and line.
logs --follow --json redacts secret-looking output before streaming.
clean --safe removes configured safe cache paths while preserving running logs, latest failed logs, and failure-linked event evidence.
status --all active-run next actions retain stable project-id logs/stop targets while stale reconcile next actions preserve alias behavior.

must_preserve_evidence.MP-012: JSON envelopes remain single-object for ordinary --json commands; logs --follow --json and events --follow --json use JSON Lines.
must_preserve_evidence.MP-015: Secret-looking event data and streamed log lines are redacted before persistence/output.
must_preserve_evidence.running_logs: Safe clean protects active run log paths discovered from local runtime state and global run index.
must_preserve_evidence.failure_evidence: Safe clean protects failed/stop_failed/stale run log paths, failure-linked event data.logPath values, and failed log filename fallbacks.

CA-CP-010.evidence: events command reads append-only JSONL history; malformed event parsing behavior remains in events store tests.

CA-CP-016.evidence: redactLogLine covers secret-looking streamed log output; event redaction remains applied before persistence.

CA-CP-019.evidence: ordinary --json command envelopes continue through createSuccessEnvelope/createFailureEnvelope; stream follow output emits JSON Lines only for follow.
